entries.title: Please, We Beg, Just One Weekend Free Of Appliances (Citrix NetScaler CVE-2026-3055 Memory Overread Part 2)
entries.summary: Today, we woke up with a nagging feeling: what if Citrix had, in fact, patched multiple Memory Overread vulnerabilities as part of CVE-2026-3055? entries.title: The Sequels Are Never As Good, But We're Still In Pain (Citrix NetScaler CVE-2026-3055 Memory Overread)
entries.summary: Sequels? Pain? We're obviously talking about Citrix NetScalers, yet again. Welcome back to another watchTowr Labs blog post - pull up a chair, we always welcome new members to our group therapy sessions. entries.title: A 32-Year-Old Bug Walks Into A Telnet Server (GNU inetutils Telnetd CVE-2026-32746 Pre-Auth RCE)
entries.summary: A long, long time ago, in a land free of binary exploit mitigations, when Unix still roamed the Earth, there lived a pre-authentication Telnetd vulnerability. In fact, this vulnerability was born so long ago (way back in 1994) that it may even be older than you. To put the timespan

entries.title: The Most Organized Threat Actors Use Your ITSM (BMC FootPrints Pre-Auth Remote Code Execution Chains)
entries.summary: SolarWinds. Ivanti. SysAid. ManageEngine. Giants of the KEV world, all of whom have ITSM side-projects. ITSMs, as a group of solutions, have played pivotal roles in numerous ransomware gang campaigns - not only do they represent code running on a system, but they hold a significant amount of sensitive information.

entries.title: Sometimes, You Can Just Feel The Security In The Design (Juniper Junos Evolved CVE-2026-21902 Pre-Auth RCE)
entries.summary: On today’s ‘good news disguised as other things’ segment, we’re turning our gaze to CVE-2026-21902 - a recently disclosed “Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource” vulnerability affecting Juniper’s Junos OS Evolved platform. entries.title: Buy A Help Desk, Bundle A Remote Access Solution? (SolarWinds Web Help Desk Pre-Auth RCE Chain(s))

entries.title: Someone Knows Bash Far Too Well, And We Love It (Ivanti EPMM Pre-Auth RCEs CVE-2026-1281 & CVE-2026-1340)
entries.summary: When Ivanti removed the embargoes from CVE-2026-1281 and CVE-2026-1340 - actively exploited pre-auth Remote Command Execution vulnerabilities in Ivanti’s Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) solution - we sighed with relief. entries.title: Attackers With Decompilers Strike Again (SmarterTools SmarterMail WT-2026-0001 Auth Bypass)
entries.summary: Well, well, well - look what we’re back with. You may recall that merely two weeks ago, we analyzed CVE-2025-52691 - a pre-auth RCE vulnerability in the SmarterTools SmarterMail email solution with a timeline that is typically reserved for KEV hall-of-famers. entries.summary: Welcome to 2026! entries.title: SOAPwn: Pwning .NET Framework Applications Through HTTP Client Proxies And WSDL
entries.summary: Welcome back! As we near the end of 2025, we are, of course, waiting for the next round of SSLVPN exploitation to occur in January (as it did in 2024 and 2025). Weeeeeeeee. Before then, we want to clear the decks and see how much research we can publish. This

entries.title: Stop Putting Your Passwords Into Random Websites (Yes, Seriously, You Are The Problem)
entries.summary: Welcome to watchTowr vs the Internet, part 68. That feeling you’re experiencing? Dread. You should be used to it by now.
